message:
feat(fnp-python): native array_equiv via broadcast + elementwise_binary (br-franken_numpy-hswb)

Replace the np.array_equiv passthrough. Native path:

1. extract_precise_numeric_array on both a1, a2 — fallback for
   complex/integer-sidecar/non-numeric inputs so object/string/structured
   surfaces stay exact.
2. broadcast_shapes probe — a non-broadcastable pair returns False
   directly (matches numpy's behavior; np.array_equiv never raises on
   shape mismatch).
3. elementwise_binary(BinaryOp::Equal) broadcasts and emits a bool
   array; `all(values != 0.0)` collapses to the Python bool return
   type numpy promises.

Test array_equiv_matches_numpy_across_broadcast_scalar_object_and_mismatch
passes; full fnp-python suite: 395/395 lib tests green on rch.

k74v.1 / k74v.2 scope note: a grep of every name listed in those beads
shows all 70 ufunc wrappers and ~96 function wrappers are already
registered (via `py_abs`/`py_mod` for Rust-keyword names). The four
P2 entries that don't appear (`get_array_wrap`, `lookfor`,
`maximum_sctype`, `source`) are numpy 2.0 removals, not omissions.
